description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Missouri (see pronunciation) is a landlocked state in the Midwestern region of the United States.

description: city in and county seat of Garfield County, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Enid ( EE-nid) is the ninth-largest city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: city in Scott County, Kansas, United States of America
description_wiki: Scott City is a city in and the county seat of Scott County, Kansas, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Sumner County, Kansas, United States
description_wiki: Wellington is a city in and the county seat of Sumner County, Kansas, United States.

description: public university system in Colorado, USA
description_wiki: The University of Colorado (CU) is a system of public universities in Colorado.

entity: Supreme Court of the United States
surface_forms: Supreme Court Justice of the United States of America
aliases: Supreme Court of the United States of America
description: highest court of jurisdiction in the United States of America
description_wiki: The Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) is the highest court in the federal judiciary of the United States.

description: public university in Wichita, Kansas, U.S.
American aircraft designer (1879-1954)
description_wiki: Clyde Vernon Cessna (; December 5, 1879 – November 20, 1954) was an American aircraft designer, aviator, and early aviation entrepreneur.

description: American pioneer aviator; co-founder of Beech Aircraft Company (1891-1950)
description_wiki: Walter Herschel Beech (January 30, 1891 – November 29, 1950) was an American aviator and early aviation entrepreneur who co-founded the Beech Aircraft Company (now called Beechcraft) in 1932 with his wife, Olive Ann Beech, and a team of three others.

description: aircraft manufacturer in the United States
description_wiki: Beechcraft is an American brand of civil aviation and military aircraft owned by Textron Aviation since 2014, headquartered in Wichita, Kansas.

description: city in and county seat of Sedgwick County, Kansas, United States
description_wiki: Wichita ( , WITCH-ih-taw) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Kansas and the county seat of Sedgwick County.

description: air warfare branch of the U.S. Army from 1926 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Corps (USAAC) was the aerial warfare service component of the United States Army between 1926 and 1941.

description: aerial warfare branch of the United States Army from 1941 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Forces (USAAF or AAF) was the major land-based aerial warfare service component of the United States Army and de facto aerial warfare service branch of the United States during and immediately after World War II (1941–1947).

description: family of military training aircraft
description_wiki: The Fairchild PT-19 (company designation Fairchild M62) is an American  monoplane primary trainer aircraft that served with the United States Army Air Forces, RAF and RCAF during World War II. Designed by Fairchild Aircraft, it was a contemporary of the Kaydet biplane trainer, and was used by the USAAF during Primary Flying Training.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

description: aircraft manufacturer in the United States
description_wiki: Beechcraft is an American brand of civil aviation and military aircraft owned by Textron Aviation since 2014, headquartered in Wichita, Kansas.

description: university in Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: The University of Tulsa (TU) is a private research university in Tulsa, Oklahoma, United States.

description: American college football bowl game
description_wiki: The Orange Bowl is an annual American college football bowl game played in the Miami metropolitan area.

description: consolidated city-county and capital of Colorado, United States
description_wiki: Denver  (  DEN-vər) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Colorado.

description: city in Oklahoma, United States and county seat of Tulsa County
description_wiki: Tulsa (  TUL-sə) is the second-most-populous city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma and the 48th-most populous city in the United States.

description: public holiday in the United States
description_wiki: Labor Day is a federal holiday in the United States celebrated on the first Monday of September to honor and recognize the American labor movement and the works and contributions of laborers to the development and achievements in the United States.

description: historic building in Tulsa, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: The Philtower Building is a historic building located at 427 South Boston Avenue in Tulsa, Oklahoma.

description: former American oil company, current brand of BP
former American oil company, current brand of BP
description_wiki: Amoco ( AM-ə-koh) is a brand of fuel stations operating in the United States and owned by British conglomerate BP since 1998.

description: American multinational oil and gas corporation
description_wiki: Exxon Mobil Corporation ( EK-son MOH-bəl) is an American multinational oil and gas corporation headquartered in Spring, Texas, a suburb of Houston.

description: airport in Tulsa, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Tulsa International Airport (IATA: TUL, ICAO: KTUL, FAA LID: TUL) is a civil-military airport five miles (8 km) northeast of Downtown Tulsa, in Tulsa County, Oklahoma, United States.

description: American oil industrialist and businessman (1878-1957)
description_wiki: William Grove Skelly (June 10, 1878 – April 11, 1957) was an American entrepreneur who made a fortune in the oil business.

description: American industrialist and art collector (1892–1976)
description_wiki: Jean Paul Getty Sr.

description: 1929-1994 aerospace manufacturer
description_wiki: The Grumman Aircraft Engineering Corporation, later Grumman Aerospace Corporation, was a 20th century American producer of military and civilian aircraft.

description: aircraft manufacturer in the United States
1926-1995 aerospace manufacturer in the United States
description_wiki: The Lockheed Corporation was an American aerospace manufacturer.

description: American global aerospace and defense corporation
description_wiki: The Boeing Company ( BO-ing) is an American multinational corporation that designs and manufactures airplanes, rotorcraft, rockets, satellites, and missiles worldwide.

description: American manufacturing company
description_wiki: The Martin Marietta Corporation was an American company founded in 1961 through the merger of Glenn L. Martin Company and American-Marietta Corporation.
